{"id":"smallhold-mic6","title":"CLI reindex command: smallhold search reindex","description":"## What
Implement the `smallhold search reindex` CLI subcommand that drops and rebuilds the tantivy index from all posts in the DB, with progress output and safety guards.

## Why
After schema migrations, index corruption, or index directory loss, operators need a reliable offline tool to restore search without restarting or patching the server.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read tantivy docs for dropping an index directory and recreating it (schema must match); understand how to acquire an exclusive lock on the index directory before deletion (tantivy's Directory::lock or simply checking for active writers via a lockfile); read clap v4 derive docs for adding a subcommand with an optional --yes flag for non-interactive confirmation.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add `search reindex` as a clap subcommand under the admin CLI; the command opens SQLite directly (no running server required); checks that search_index_path is writable (exit nonzero with error if not); acquires tantivy's DirectoryLock on the index dir (exit with clear error if another process holds it); deletes all files in the index directory; creates a fresh index with the same schema; iterates all posts from DB in batches of 500, printing progress ("Indexed N/Total posts..."); calls IndexWriter::commit after each batch; prints "Reindex complete. N posts indexed." on success; exits nonzero on any error.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write an integration test that indexes 5 posts, manually corrupts the index directory (delete one segment file), runs reindex, then searches for a word from one of the 5 posts and asserts the result is found — confirming reindex restored search correctly.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: after reindex, search result is verified against the known post id from the DB INSERT return value — not re-derived from index internals.
- Test approach: the test is self-contained: seed DB, corrupt index, invoke reindex as a library function (not subprocess), search, assert result.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths (CLI exits are OK with process::exit, but prefer returning Result and printing error)
- Acquire exclusive lock on index directory before deleting — prevents concurrent server index access during rebuild
- Exit nonzero and print a clear error message if index directory is not writable
- Reindex runs as a CLI command (talks to SQLite directly) — the server does not need to be running
- Print progress to stdout so operators can see it is not hung on large datasets
- Batch size of 500 with per-batch commit avoids holding all posts in memory at once
- On any batch error, log the error and continue (best-effort reindex) or abort and print instructions — document which behavior is chosen","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T04:21:16Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:21:16Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-i28n","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:21:21Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
